---
title: "Health Statistics From A Geographer's Perspective"
sidebar: false
resources:
  - slides.pdf
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<div class="project-pdf-slides-root" hidden aria-hidden="true"></div>

::: {.project-pdf-slides-intro}

This presentation introduces medical geography through a practical, data-driven lens, highlighting how geographic concepts, spatial analysis, and visualization techniques can be applied to understand patterns in health outcomes and health-related behaviours. Drawing on examples from urban, suburban, and rural contexts, the session demonstrates how geographers use tools such as geocoding, spatial joins, clustering, autocorrelation, and advanced modelling approaches - including geographically weighted regression and geographical random forests - to uncover spatial inequalities, identify at-risk populations, and support public health decision-making. The goal is to expand awareness of what medical geography can offer and to inspire new ideas for applying spatial thinking to contemporary health challenges.

:::

::: {.pdf-slides-embed-bar}
[Open PDF in new tab](slides.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
:::

::: {#pdf-slides-frame-wrap .pdf-slides-embed-full}
<iframe src="slides.pdf" title="Medical geography presentation (PDF)" class="pdf-slides-embed"></iframe>
:::
